steps.title: Identify the student's causal claim
steps.content: The student concludes that IGF-1 gene variations “must account for” the observed size differences among dog breeds—claiming these variations are the responsible cause.

steps.title: Find what contradicts this
steps.content: Choice A directly contradicts this by stating that the IGF-1 gene is “one of multiple genes known to affect the size of dogs.” If other genes also drive size differences, then IGF-1 variations cannot alone account for all the variance. The other choices either affirm the claim (C confirms that IGF-1 variations do affect size variance), address a specific variant's prevalence without challenging the gene's explanatory power (B), or discuss applicability to other species unrelated to explaining dog size (D). That matches choice A — A is correct.
